name: Multi-agent AI press room to research, draft and review WordPress articles

parameters.content:
## Multi-agent AI press room to research, draft and review WordPress articles

### How it works

Send a brief via webhook and a three-agent pipeline handles the rest. A researcher agent outlines the article and plans a featured image. A writer agent drafts the full HTML post. A reviewer agent edits for voice, structure and factual accuracy. Imagen 4 generates a 16:9 featured image. The result is published as a WordPress draft with Rank Math SEO fields set, and a Slack notification is sent with a link to the draft for review.

Each agent can use a different LLM provider — mix Claude, Gemini, Mistral or OpenAI per stage in the CONFIG node.

**Activate the workflow** then POST to `/webhook/article-publisher`:
```
{ "prompt": "Write an article about..." }
```
Optional body fields: `category_id`, `tone_guide`, `style_guide`

### Setup

**Step 1 — Import the LLM helper sub-workflow**

This workflow calls [Route AI prompts to Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, or OpenAI](https://n8n.io/workflows/16330) as a sub-workflow for all LLM requests. Import and activate it first, then update the `CALL RESEARCHER`, `CALL WRITER` and `CALL REVIEWER` nodes to reference your imported copy.

**Step 2 — Update the CONFIG node** with your:
- `WORDPRESS_URL` — your WordPress site URL
- `DEFAULT_CATEGORY_ID` — default category ID for new posts (1 = Uncategorized)
- `AUTHOR_ID` — WordPress user ID to assign as post author
- `SLACK_CHANNEL` — Slack channel ID for completion notifications (leave blank to disable)
- `ENABLE_IMAGE_GENERATION` — set to `false` to skip Imagen 4 image generation
- `ENABLE_INTERNAL_LINKS` — set to `false` to skip fetching recent posts for internal linking
- `IMAGE_STYLE_PROMPT` — style prefix prepended to every Imagen 4 prompt
- `TONE_GUIDE` — writing voice and persona for the agents (overridable per request)
- `STYLE_GUIDE` — article structure and formatting rules (overridable per request)
- `WEBHOOK_SECRET` — optional Bearer token to secure the endpoint (leave blank to disable)

**Agent model configuration** — set provider and model for each stage independently:
- `AGENT_RESEARCHER_PROVIDER` / `AGENT_RESEARCHER_MODEL` — outline and research (default: `anthropic` / `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
- `AGENT_WRITER_PROVIDER` / `AGENT_WRITER_MODEL` — full draft (default: `google` / `gemini-3.5-flash`)
- `AGENT_REVIEWER_PROVIDER` / `AGENT_REVIEWER_MODEL` — editorial review (default: `anthropic` / `claude-sonnet-4-6`)

To use a single model for all three stages, set all six fields to the same provider and model. Supported providers: `anthropic`, `google`, `mistral`, `openai`.

**Step 3 — Link credentials** in the nodes listed:
- Google AI (PaLM) API → `GENERATE IMAGE`
- WordPress API → `FETCH RECENT POSTS`, `UPLOAD IMAGE TO WORDPRESS`, `SET IMAGE ALT TEXT`, `UPSERT TAG`, `CREATE WORDPRESS POST`, `SET RANK MATH SEO`
- Slack OAuth2 → `SLACK NOTIFICATION`, `SLACK PIPELINE ERROR`

*(LLM credentials are configured in the sub-workflow, not here.)*

### Customization

**(Optional) Rank Math SEO:** Requires the Rank Math plugin plus a custom mu-plugin to expose `rank_math_*` meta fields to the REST API. Place `rank-math-rest.php` in `/wp-content/mu-plugins/`. Full plugin code is in the Rank Math SEO sticky note on the canvas.

### Image Generation (optional)

Set `ENABLE_IMAGE_GENERATION` to `false` in CONFIG to skip.

When enabled, Google Imagen 4 generates a 16:9 featured image.
The filename and alt text are derived from the focus keyword.

Requires a Google AI (PaLM) API credential with Imagen 4 access.

assignments.name: IMAGE_STYLE_PROMPT
assignments.value: Replace with your preferred image style. This prefix is prepended to every Imagen 4 prompt. Example: Retro tech lab illustration, flat design, clean lines, no text, landscape orientation, subject:

assignments.name: TONE_GUIDE
assignments.value:
Write in first person as the site author.

Replace this guide with a description of your own writing voice, audience, and goals. Example structure:

Voice: [describe your preferred tone — e.g. direct and warm, technical but accessible, never salesy]

Frame content as: [e.g. here is what I tried, here is what I found, here is how you might apply it]

Reader: [describe your target audience — their technical level, what they are trying to do, what they already know]

[Your site name] is [brief description — what it is, what it is for, what tone reflects it best].

assignments.name: STYLE_GUIDE
assignments.value:
Structure every article as follows:

- Opening paragraph: the first sentence should carry weight on its own. 

- H2 sections for main topics, H3 for subsections where needed. 

- Use bullet lists sparingly — only for genuine lists of 3+ parallel items. Never use bullets to avoid writing proper sentences.

- Tables for comparisons, not bullets.

- Closing paragraph: one concrete takeaway or honest assessment. End on something useful or thought-provoking.

- Figures and estimates: for any specific figure — a percentage, price, benchmark, timing, or statistic — either (a) name the source inline, or (b) clearly label it as a personal observation or rough estimate. Do not state estimates as established facts.

- Do not include specific pricing figures for third-party services. Reference the service's pricing page instead.

- Ideal length: 800–1400 words for most articles. Go longer only if the topic genuinely requires it.

workflowId.cachedResultName: Route AI prompts to Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, or OpenAI

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "instances": [{ "prompt": $("CONFIG").item.json.IMAGE_STYLE_PROMPT + $("PARSE OUTLINE").item.json.outline.image_subject + ". No text. No words. No letters." }], "parameters": { "sampleCount": 1, "aspectRatio": "16:9" } } }}

workflowId.cachedResultName: Route AI prompts to Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, or OpenAI

parameters.jsCode:
const cfg = $('CONFIG').item.json; const draftRaw = $input.item.json.llm_response || ''; const draftStripped = draftRaw .replace(/^```[a-z]*\s*/i, '') .replace(/```\s*$/i, '') .trim(); const excerptMatch = draftStripped.match(/EXCERPT:\s*([\s\S]+?)(?: [\s\S]*|$)/i); const excerpt = excerptMatch ? excerptMatch[1].trim() : ''; const draftContent = draftStripped.replace(/ *EXCERPT:[\s\S]*/i, '').trim(); const outline = $('PARSE OUTLINE').item.json.outline; const originalPrompt = $('PREPARE INPUT').item.json.prompt; const provider = (cfg.AGENT_REVIEWER_PROVIDER || 'anthropic').toLowerCase(); const model = cfg.AGENT_REVIEWER_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6'; const modelKey = provider === 'google' ? 'google_model' : provider === 'mistral' ? 'mistral_model' : 'anthropic_model'; return [{ json: { llm_provider: provider, [modelKey]: model, response_format: 'text', max_tokens: 12000, temperature: 0.3, _draft_content: draftContent, _draft_excerpt: excerpt, _outline: outline, systemPrompt: 'IMPORTANT: Begin your response immediately with the opening HTML tag of the revised article (

or

). Do not output any analysis, reasoning, checklists, voice checks, notes, or commentary before the HTML content. Your internal review process must not appear in the output. You are a technical editor and voice guardian. Your job is to ensure every post reflects the author's voice consistently — not a content agency tone, not a corporate blog, and not generic AI output. Review the draft against the brief and the voice criteria in the tone guide, then rewrite any sections that fail. Output TWO sections separated by the exact line ---JSON--- Section 1: the revised full HTML article body (no wrapping tags, no preamble, no commentary — start directly with

or

). Section 2: a JSON object on a single line with these exact fields: - title (string) - excerpt (two sentences, string) - tags (string[]) - seo_title (string, MUST be under 60 characters AND must contain the focus_keyword verbatim — write this as a keyword-forward search snippet, not a copy of the post title) - seo_description (string, MUST be under 155 characters AND must contain the focus_keyword verbatim) - focus_keyword (string — a short 2–4 word phrase that naturally describes the topic) - image_subject (string, one-sentence visual scene) - affiliate_flag (string — MUST be a plain-English description of affiliate opportunities in this article, or the string "none" if there are none. NEVER output a boolean true or false.) VOICE CHECKLIST — apply these silently while editing. Do not output the checklist or any evaluation notes: 1. OPENING — Read the first sentence. If it functions only as personal backstory or throat-clearing rather than starting at the problem or observation, rewrite it. The first sentence should carry weight on its own. 2. No corporate filler phrases ("highly capable", "seamlessly integrates", "robust solution", "leverage"). 3. Apply the language conventions and voice described in the tone guide throughout. 4. No fabricated statistics — either name the source inline or label it as a personal estimate. 5. No third-party pricing figures — reference the service's pricing page instead. 6. Closing paragraph: one concrete takeaway or honest assessment. No "In conclusion" or summary restatement.', userPrompt: 'Original brief: ' + originalPrompt + ' Outline title: ' + (outline.title || '') + ' Focus keyword: ' + (outline.focus_keyword || '') + ' Tone guide: ' + cfg.TONE_GUIDE + ' DRAFT: ' + draftContent + ' ' + (excerpt ? 'EXCERPT FROM DRAFT: ' + excerpt : '') + ' Review and revise. Begin your response immediately with

or

— no preamble, no notes, no checklist output. Output the revised HTML body, then ---JSON--- on its own line, then the metadata JSON object.' } }];

workflowId.cachedResultName: Route AI prompts to Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, or OpenAI

parameters.jsCode:
const tags = $('PARSE ARTICLE').first().json.tags || [];
return tags.map(tag => ({ json: { tag_name: tag } }));

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "title": $("PARSE ARTICLE").first().json.title, "content": $("PARSE ARTICLE").first().json.content, "excerpt": $("PARSE ARTICLE").first().json.excerpt, "status": "draft", "slug": $("PARSE ARTICLE").first().json.focus_keyword.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, ''), "categories": [Number($("PREPARE INPUT").first().json.category_id)], "tags": $("COLLECT TAG IDS").first().json.tag_ids, "featured_media": $("MERGE IMAGE RESULT").first().json.media_id, "author": Number($("CONFIG").first().json.AUTHOR_ID) } }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "error": "Pipeline failed at stage: " + $('HANDLE PIPELINE FAILURE').item.json.stage, "detail": $('HANDLE PIPELINE FAILURE').item.json.error } }}
